creative_brief: Clean, minimal design. Hero image of real produce at the Brookvale hub — soil on carrots, natural light. Single green CTA button mid-email. No stock photography. Warm, urgent tone without countdown gimmicks.
subject: The window is smaller than you think
preheader: Once the Birchal campaign fills, there's no second round at this price.
body:
Last Tuesday, I watched a mum at our Brookvale hub load three bags of regenerative produce into her car. Carrots still flecked with soil. Eggs from a farm she could name.

She paused and said something I haven't stopped thinking about: "Why did it take this long for someone to build this?"

Honest answer? Because the big chains have spent decades making sure nobody could.

FarmThru exists to prove them wrong. We're a farm-direct grocery store — no middlemen, no wholesalers inflating the price between the farmer and your family. You order online, collect from our Brookvale hub (or get delivery across Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong), and pay less for food that's actually worth eating.

Right now, we're raising equity through Birchal. And here's the part that matters if you've been watching from the sidelines:

The minimum is $50. The cap is $10,000 per investor. When it fills, it's done.

We're not a pitch deck and a dream. We have a working hub. We have 15-20 partner farms and suppliers across NSW. We have families ordering bulk produce every week — no lock-in, no ongoing fees, just real food priced for real budgets.

But we can't hold spots. Birchal campaigns don't reopen once they close. There's no waitlist after the cap.

If you've been considering it, the smartest move is the simplest one: secure your $5 refundable deposit now. That gets you VIP access — first in line when the campaign goes live, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A before the public sees a thing.

Five dollars. Fully refundable if you change your mind. But it puts you ahead of everyone still thinking about it.

→ Reserve your VIP spot for $5 (refundable)

I can't promise returns. No one honestly can. What I can tell you is that this model works, the food is real, and the people buying it keep coming back.

That window won't stay open.

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
